name: generate_figma_design
description: Capture, import, or convert a web page (by URL) or HTML into a Figma design. Use this tool when the user wants to send, import, capture, screenshot, or push a webpage to Figma. Works with both local dev servers (localhost) and external websites. For LOCAL projects: explore the user's codebase to identify the page, dev server command, and URL before calling. For EXTERNAL URLs (not localhost): use Playwright MCP to capture — do NOT use 'open' with hash fragments on external sites. Call this tool without outputMode first to get detailed capture instructions and choose output mode. After capture, poll with captureId every 5 seconds (up to 10 times) until status is 'completed'. Each capture ID is single-use.

captureId.description: ID returned from initial call to check capture status. If provided, polls for completion. Each capture ID is single-use and can only capture one page. To capture multiple pages, either: (1) use one capture ID for one page and let the user manually navigate to other pages via the capture toolbar (which auto-generates new capture IDs), or (2) call this tool once per page to generate a separate capture ID for each.

fileName.description: Only include this if outputMode is 'newFile'. The name of the Figma file to generate. Never ask the user for this, just auto-generate it.

planKey.description: Only include this if outputMode is 'newFile'.  The team or organization key where the generated design file should be saved. If not provided, returns a list of available plans to choose from.

outputMode.description: How to output the captured design. "newFile" creates a new Figma file. "existingFile" adds to an existing file. "clipboard" copies the design to clipboard for pasting. If omitted, the tool returns options for the user to choose from.

fileKey.description: Only include this if outputMode is 'existingFile'. The key of the target Figma file. Can be extracted from a Figma URL like "figma.com/design/{fileKey}/..." or from a claim URL like "figma.com/integrations/claim/{fileKey}". When polling a completed newFile capture, the file_key is provided in the response. Note: If the file was just created via a claim URL, the user must open the claim URL first to claim the file before it can be edited with existingFile mode.

nodeId.description: Only include this if outputMode is 'existingFile'. Optional node ID where the design should be added. If not provided, creates a new page in the file. Format: "123:456"
